title: Basic closure
instruction:
A closure is written as `|params| body`.

Run this.
successMessage: Closures infer their parameter and return types. double infers |x: i32| -> i32. add infers |a: i32, b: i32| -> i32.

title: Capturing by reference
instruction:
Closures capture variables from their environment.

Run this.
successMessage: is_big captures threshold by reference. The closure can use it as long as threshold is in scope.

title: Capturing by value with move
instruction:
`move` forces the closure to take ownership of captured variables.

Necessary for closures used in threads.

Run this.
successMessage: move transfers ownership of msg into the closure. The closure can be called multiple times and msg is available each time.

title: Closures as arguments
instruction:
Pass closures to functions that take `impl Fn`, `impl FnMut`, or `impl FnOnce`.

Run this.
successMessage: F: Fn(i32)->i32 is a trait bound for closures and functions. You can pass named closures, anonymous closures, or named functions.

title: Returning closures
instruction:
Return a closure with `impl Fn` (when the type can be inferred) or `Box<dyn Fn>`.

Run this.
successMessage: make_adder returns a closure that captures n. Each returned closure remembers its own n. This is a function factory.

title: FnOnce — consume captured data
instruction:
`FnOnce` closures can only be called once because they consume their captured data.

Run this.
successMessage: FnOnce is for closures that consume their captures. Rust's type system prevents calling them twice.

title: Closures and iterators
instruction:
Closures shine with iterator methods like map, filter, and fold.

Run this.
successMessage: filter keeps evens, map squares each. collect turns the iterator into Vec. This functional pipeline is concise and expressive.

title: Mutable closure with FnMut
instruction:
`FnMut` closures can mutate captured variables. They can be called multiple times.

Run this.
successMessage: The closure captures count mutably and increments it each call. FnMut allows this — the closure can modify its captured environment.
